skill.name: data-analysis
skill.description: Analyze tabular data with Python, generate pandas analysis code, and create professional matplotlib and seaborn visualizations.
skill.summary: Generate Python data analysis scripts and publication-ready charts from a dataset context.
skill.author: BOHUYESHAN-APB

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution and weak-crypto findings were false positives caused by Markdown fences, code-fence parsing, and ordinary descriptive text. The skill still has medium risk because it sends user-provided data context to an OpenAI-compatible endpoint and returns generated Python that is intended for sandbox execution.

medium_findings.title: Generated Python Code Intended For Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill asks an LLM to generate complete Python scripts and returns extracted code blocks. The SKILL.md file states this code can be executed in a sandbox, so unsafe generated code could affect files, data, or network access if the sandbox is not strict.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code generation flow and sandbox execution intent are explicit. Risk depends on the caller sandbox, so this is elevated but not confirmed malicious.

medium_findings.title: User Data Sent To Configurable OpenAI Endpoint
medium_findings.description: The generator sends data_context and requirement into an OpenAI chat completion request. The API base URL can come from configuration or environment variables, so sensitive dataset details may leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The client creation and completion request are directly visible. There is no evidence of covert exfiltration, but the data flow to an external endpoint is real.

low_findings.title: Environment Secret Access For API Client
low_findings.description: The skill reads OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_BASE_URL from configuration or environment variables. This is a normal client setup pattern, but users should understand that credentials are required and used for model calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment access is explicit and limited to OpenAI client configuration. I did not find evidence that the key is logged or sent outside the configured client.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static shell-execution findings point to Markdown code fences and Python string splitting around code fences. I did not find shell command execution at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain literal backticks inside prompts, documentation, or string parsing. They do not invoke a shell or subprocess.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto detections on SKILL.md lines 4, 14, and 28 do not reference cryptographic algorithms. They are ordinary prose about data analysis and sandbox usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no cryptographic API, algorithm name, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive implementation.

dangerous_patterns.title: LLM Output Used As Executable Python
dangerous_patterns.description: The generator extracts a Python code block from model output and returns it. Downstream execution must occur only inside a restricted sandbox with no secrets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The extraction logic is explicit, and the skill documentation describes sandbox execution. The risk is controlled only by the external sandbox.

dangerous_patterns.title: Configurable External Model Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The OpenAI-compatible base URL can be supplied by configuration or environment variables. A malicious or mistaken endpoint could receive dataset context and prompts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The base URL selection and completion call are visible. No allowlist or endpoint validation appears in the reviewed lines.

content.value_statement: Data teams often need fast exploratory analysis and clear visual summaries. This skill generates Python analysis scripts and styled charts using pandas, matplotlib, and seaborn.
actual_capabilities: Generates complete Python scripts for dataset analysis tasks.
Uses pandas and NumPy for cleaning, filtering, and aggregation.
Creates matplotlib and seaborn charts such as bar, line, scatter, pie, and heatmap views.
Applies a reusable professional chart style with a defined color palette.
Extracts Python code from model responses for downstream sandbox execution.
limitations: It depends on an OpenAI-compatible API key and model endpoint.
Generated code must be reviewed before execution outside a strict sandbox.
It does not validate dataset quality or schema before generating code.
It does not include built-in privacy filtering for sensitive data context.

use_cases.title: Explore Product Metrics
use_cases.description: Generate pandas analysis and charts for conversion, retention, or usage data.

use_cases.title: Prepare Research Visuals
use_cases.description: Create clean charts from experiment results or survey exports.

use_cases.title: Automate Report Charts
use_cases.description: Draft repeatable Python scripts that save polished chart images for reports.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize A Dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this dataset context and generate a Python script that summarizes key fields, missing values, and useful grouped metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Single Chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Python script that creates one clear chart for this requirement and saves it as chart_output.png.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Segments
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Python script that compares these customer segments, calculates the most important metrics, and creates a readable comparison chart.

prompt_templates.title: Build A Reproducible Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete Python analysis script with cleaning steps, grouped calculations, assumptions, and a final visualization for this business question.

output_examples.input: A sales CSV has date, region, product, revenue, and units columns. Show monthly revenue by region.
output_examples.output: A Python analysis script that parses dates, groups revenue by month and region, and saves a styled line chart.

output_examples.input: A survey export has satisfaction scores, user plan, and response date. Compare satisfaction by plan.
output_examples.output: A script that cleans missing scores, calculates plan averages, and produces a labeled bar chart.

output_examples.input: A support dataset has ticket category, priority, resolution hours, and created date. Find slow categories.
output_examples.output: A script that computes resolution summaries by category and priority, then saves a heatmap for review.

best_practices: Remove sensitive fields before sending dataset context to the model endpoint.
Run generated scripts only in a restricted sandbox with minimal file and network access.
Review chart labels, aggregation logic, and assumptions before publishing results.
anti_patterns: Do not execute generated code directly on production systems.
Do not include raw secrets, personal data, or private records in the data context.
Do not treat generated analysis as final without checking calculations.

faq.question: What data tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: It is designed around Python, pandas, NumPy, matplotlib, and seaborn.

faq.question: Does it execute the generated analysis code?
faq.answer: The generator returns code. Execution should happen only in a separate restricted sandbox.

faq.question: Can it create charts for reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It instructs generated scripts to save a chart image named chart_output.png.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The code reads an OpenAI API key from configuration or environment variables.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive datasets?
faq.answer: Use caution. Dataset context is sent to the configured model endpoint.

faq.question: Can it match a document theme?
faq.answer: It provides a helper style function with a professional color palette for charts.
